name: US Cash Advance
description_short: US Cash Advance offers payday loans, cash advances, and bad credit loans up to $50,000 with same-day funding at their Detroit location and online.
description_long:
US Cash Advance operates a physical storefront at 20002 Schoolcraft Rd in Detroit, Michigan, and provides online lending services for consumers needing quick access to cash. The company specializes in short-term emergency loans including payday loans (up to $1,000), cash advances, bad credit loans (up to $50,000), and same-day funding options. According to their website, they accept all credit types and do not require collateral for their unsecured loan products. The company appears to connect borrowers with third-party lenders through their platform rather than funding loans directly.

Their service offerings include payday loans, cash advances, personal loans for bad credit, same-day loans, quick loans, loans without credit checks, debt consolidation products, and cash loans. Loan amounts range from $100 to $50,000 depending on product type. The Detroit location operates Monday-Friday 8am-9pm, Saturday-Sunday 10am-7pm, with phone support at (313) 307-3785. They advertise a streamlined process involving an online inquiry form, connection with a lender, offer review, contract signing, and cash disbursement.

What distinguishes US Cash Advance is their emphasis on speed and accessibility for borrowers with poor credit. Customer reviews highlight fast in-person processing (under 45 minutes reported), same-day funding capabilities, transparent terms, and patient staff. One review specifically praised staff member Doris for making the process easy and answering all questions. The company advertises benefits including no collateral requirement, minimum paperwork, and the ability to use borrowed funds for any purpose.

However, important caveats apply. The website content is sparse on critical borrower protections and cost disclosures—annual percentage rates, fees, and repayment terms are not detailed. One review mentioned the company as "Express Payday Loans" under a different context, raising potential confusion about branding or affiliate relationships. The business model relies on third-party lender connections rather than direct lending. Consumers should verify all terms, APR, and fees before signing any contract, as payday loans typically carry high interest rates and short repayment windows.
address: 10404 S 15th St unit A, Bellevue, NE 68123

company_info.headquarters: 10404 S 15th St unit A, Bellevue, NE 68123

pros: Same-day and next-day funding available for emergency cash needs
Accepts all credit types including bad credit applicants
Fast in-person processing reported at under 45 minutes
Loan amounts range from $100 to $50,000 depending on product type
No collateral required for unsecured loan products
Extended hours (8am-9pm weekdays, 10am-7pm weekends) at physical location
cons: APR, fees, and interest rates not disclosed on website—critical cost information missing
No information about repayment terms, loan duration, or late payment penalties
Relies on third-party lenders rather than direct lending, potentially limiting transparency
Customer review mentions company as 'Express Payday Loans' in different context, creating branding confusion
Payday and cash advance products typically carry high interest rates and short repayment cycles
best_for: Consumers with bad credit needing emergency cash before next paycheck
Borrowers seeking same-day or next-day funding for unexpected expenses
Individuals who prefer in-person transactions with staff assistance
diagnosis: US Cash Advance is best for consumers in immediate financial emergencies who have limited credit options and need cash within hours or days, particularly those able to visit the physical Detroit location. The primary caveat is that payday and cash advance products typically carry very high APRs and short repayment terms that can trap borrowers in debt cycles; borrowers must obtain complete APR and fee information before applying, and should explore payday alternative loans or credit union options first.
